objectives: Talk about work and school schedules

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Verónica
dialogue_blocks.translation: What do you do for work?

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue_blocks.text: Soy ingeniero. Trabajo en una empresa de tecnología en el centro.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I'm an engineer. I work at a technology company downtown.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈsoj inxeˈnjeɾo ‖ tɾaˈβaxo en ˈuna emˈpɾesa ðe teknoloˈxia]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Verónica
dialogue_blocks.translation: And how many hours do you work per day?

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue_blocks.text: Normalmente ocho horas, pero a veces más. El horario es de nueve a seis.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Normally eight hours, but sometimes more. The schedule is from nine to six.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [noɾmalˈmente ˈoʧo ˈoɾas ‖ ˈpeɾo a ˈβeθes ˈmas]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue_blocks.text: Sí, mucho. Aunque a veces el jefe nos da mucha tarea.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Yes, a lot. Although sometimes the boss gives us a lot of work.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si ˈmuʧo ‖ ˈauŋke a ˈβeθes el ˈxefe nos ˈða ˈmuʧa ˈtaɾea]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Verónica
dialogue_blocks.text: ¿Y en el futuro? ¿Quieres seguir en esa empresa?
dialogue_blocks.translation: And in the future? Do you want to stay at that company?
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [i en el fuˈtuɾo ‖ ˈkjeɾes seˈɣiɾ en ˈesa emˈpɾesa]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue_blocks.text: No sé. Me gustaría montar mi propio negocio algún día.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I don't know. I'd like to start my own business someday.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [no ˈse ‖ me ɡustaˈɾia monˈtaɾ mi ˈpɾopjo neˈɡoθjo alˈɣun ˈðia]

srs_flashcards.back: What do you do for work?

exercises.question: How do you ask someone what they do for work in Mexican Spanish?

feedback.correct: ¡Correcto! '¿A qué te dedicas?' is the natural way to ask someone's profession in Mexican Spanish.
feedback.incorrect: '¿A qué te dedicas?' is the most natural Mexican Spanish way to ask about someone's profession.

exercises.instruction: Une el lugar con su descripción

pairs.english: where office workers have meetings and desks

exercises.instruction: Completa la conversación sobre el trabajo

dialogue.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue.text: Llevo tres años. El _____ es bueno y me gustan mis compañeros. (ambiente)

dialogue.speaker: Rodrigo
dialogue.text: Podría ser mejor, pero tengo _____ y vacaciones pagadas. (beneficios)

speaking_rubric.title: Mi trabajo o mis estudios
speaking_rubric.scenario: Tell someone about your job or studies — what you do, where, your schedule, and whether you enjoy it.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Describe tu trabajo o tus estudios. Menciona el horario y cómo te sientes.

sample_response.text: Soy maestro de primaria. Trabajo en una escuela pública de lunes a viernes. Me encanta mi trabajo porque los niños me dan mucha energía.
sample_response.ipa: [ˈsoj maˈestɾo ðe pɾiˈmaɾja ‖ tɾaˈβaxo en ˈuna esˈkwela ˈpuβlika]

cultural_notes.formality: In Mexico, asking '¿A qué te dedicas?' is a common early conversation topic. It's not considered invasive — it helps establish social connection.
cultural_notes.gestures: Nodding while listening to someone describe their job is important in Mexican culture — it signals active engagement.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: 'Chamba' is informal Mexican slang for job/work: '¿Cómo va la chamba?' (How's work going?). 'Chambear' means to work hard. These are very common in casual speech.
